post.description:
Production-grade Cashfree webhook handler.

Guarantees:
- Idempotent: duplicate webhooks for same order+event are safely ignored
- Race-safe: SELECT FOR UPDATE serializes concurrent handlers
- Atomic: payment status + credit addition in single DB commit
- Retryable: failed activations tracked with activation_failed status

State machine: pending -> processing -> activated | activation_failed | failed | cancelled

get.description: List payments stuck in pending (>1 hour) or activation_failed state.

Retry credit activation for a payment that succeeded at Cashfree

get.description: Health check for Fincept research module

post.description:
General-purpose LLM endpoint for running agents, processing data, and AI tasks.

This endpoint allows you to use Fincept API as your LLM provider for:
- Running AI agents
- Data processing and analysis
- Code generation
- Text summarization
- Question answering
- Any other LLM-based tasks

Cost: 5 credits per successful response
Note: Credits are ONLY deducted on successful LLM response. If the LLM fails, no credits are charged.

Rate limit: 1 request per second

Submit an LLM request for async processing.
Returns a task_id immediately. Poll /research/llm/status/{task_id} for results.

This is a drop-in addition to the existing synchronous POST /research/llm endpoint.
The synchronous endpoint stays COMPLETELY UNCHANGED.

Cost: 5 credits per successful response (same as sync endpoint)
Note: Credits are ONLY deducted on successful LLM response. If the LLM fails, no credits are charged.

Usage from client:
  1. POST /research/llm/async  { prompt, temperature, max_tokens }
     -> { success: true, task_id: "abc123" }
  2. Poll GET /research/llm/status/abc123 every 3-5 seconds
     -> { status: "processing" }  (keep polling)
     -> { status: "completed", data: { response: "...", ... } }  (done)
     -> { status: "failed", error: "..." }  (error)

Tasks auto-expire after 30 minutes.

get.description:
Poll for async LLM task status.

Returns:
  - status: "processing" -> keep polling (every 3-5 seconds)
  - status: "completed"  -> data contains the LLM response (same format as sync endpoint)
  - status: "failed"     -> error contains the failure reason

Tasks auto-expire after 30 minutes.

post.description:
Multimodal Visual Analysis using MiniMax-M2.5 for satellite imagery and visual understanding.

This endpoint enables advanced visual analysis capabilities including:
- Satellite image analysis and interpretation
- Geographic feature detection and classification
- Land use and land cover analysis
- Urban planning and infrastructure mapping
- Environmental monitoring
- Agricultural assessment
- Disaster response and damage assessment
- Object detection and counting in images
- Visual question answering
- Image description and captioning

Model: MiniMax-M2.5 (Multimodal Vision-Language Model)
- Native multimodal understanding
- 128K context window
- Advanced visual reasoning
- Function calling capabilities
- High-resolution image processing

Cost: 10 credits per successful response
Note: Credits are ONLY deducted on successful analysis. If the model fails, no credits are charged.

Rate limit: 1 request per second

Example Use Cases:
- "Identify all buildings in this satellite image"
- "What type of land use is shown in this area?"
- "Detect any changes or anomalies in this image"
- "Count the number of vehicles in this parking lot"
- "Analyze the vegetation health in this agricultural field"

get.description:
Highly configurable news events endpoint with independent filter parameters

**Cost:** 3 credits per request

**Features:**
- Filter by country, city/cities, and/or event category (all work independently)
- Get unique lists: cities, countries, or categories
- All filters work in any combination
- Pagination support (30 events per page by default)
- Returns all 10 columns of data

**Filter Parameters (all optional and independent):**
- country: Filter by country name (e.g., "India")
- city: Single city filter (e.g., "Mumbai")
- cities: Multiple cities filter (comma-separated, e.g., "Mumbai,Delhi,Bangalore")
- event_category: Filter by category

**Unique List Parameters (mutually exclusive):**
- get_unique_cities: Return unique cities list (default: false)
- get_unique_countries: Return unique countries list (default: false)
- get_unique_categories: Return unique event categories list (default: false)

**Pagination Parameters:**
- page: Page number (default: 1)
- limit: Events per page (default: 30, max: 100)

**Examples:**
- Get events: No unique parameters
- Get all cities in India: country=India&get_unique_cities=true
- Get all countries with protests: event_category=protests&get_unique_countries=true
- Get categories for Mumbai: city=Mumbai&get_unique_categories=true

**Returns:** Events with pagination OR unique list (cities/countries/categories)

parameters.name: get_unique_cities

schema.description: Return list of unique cities instead of events
schema.title: Get Unique Cities

parameters.description: Return list of unique cities instead of events

parameters.name: get_unique_countries

schema.description: Return list of unique countries instead of events
schema.title: Get Unique Countries

parameters.description: Return list of unique countries instead of events

parameters.name: get_unique_categories

schema.description: Return list of unique event categories instead of events
schema.title: Get Unique Categories

parameters.description: Return list of unique event categories instead of events

Health check for Twitter integration module

Get list of all available countries for Twitter trends
No authentication required

Get current Twitter trending topics for a country

Parameters:
- country: Country name (optional, default: "worldwide")
  Examples: "india", "usa", "uk", "japan", "brazil", "france", etc.
  Use /twitter/countries to see all available countries

Cost: 0.5 credits per request
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Get user's latest tweets (up to 20 per request)

Parameters:
- username: Twitter screen name (e.g., "elonmusk")
- user_id: Twitter user ID (recommended, more stable than username)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page

Note: Either username OR user_id is required. If both provided, user_id takes priority.

Cost: 1.0 credit per request (returns up to 20 tweets)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns tweets sorted by created_at with pagination support.

Get tweets by their IDs

Parameters:
- tweet_ids: Comma-separated tweet IDs (e.g., "1846987139428634858,1866332309399781537")

Cost: 0.5 credits per request
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns tweet data for the specified IDs.

Get replies to a tweet (up to 20 per request)

Parameters:
- tweet_id: The tweet ID to get replies for (required)
- since_time: On or after specified unix timestamp in seconds (optional)
- until_time: Before specified unix timestamp in seconds (optional)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page (optional)

Note: tweet_id must be an original tweet (not a reply to another tweet)
      and should be the first tweet in a thread.

Cost: 1.0 credit per request (returns up to 20 replies)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns replies sorted by reply time (descending) with pagination support.

Get quote tweets of a tweet (20 quotes per request)

Parameters:
- tweet_id: The tweet ID to get quotes for (required)
- since_time: On or after specified unix timestamp in seconds (optional)
- until_time: Before specified unix timestamp in seconds (optional)
- include_replies: Include replies in results (default: true)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page (optional)

Cost: 1.0 credit per request (returns 20 quotes)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns quote tweets sorted by quote time (descending) with pagination support.

Get users who retweeted a tweet (~100 users per request)

Parameters:
- tweet_id: The tweet ID to get retweeters for (required)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page (optional)

Cost: 1.5 credits per request (returns ~100 users)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns users who retweeted the tweet, sorted by retweet time (descending) with pagination support.

Get full article content from a Twitter article tweet

 PREMIUM ENDPOINT - Costs 100 credits per article!

Parameters:
- tweet_id: The tweet ID of the article (required)

Cost: 100 credits per article (PREMIUM)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns full article content including title, preview, cover image, and full text.
This is a premium endpoint due to the comprehensive article data provided.

Get the full thread context of a tweet

This endpoint retrieves the entire conversation thread containing the specified tweet.
For example, if a thread has tweets t1 -> t2 -> t3 -> t4, t5, t6 (all replying to t3),
providing t3 will return the entire thread: t1, t2, t3, t4, t5, t6.

Parameters:
- tweet_id: The tweet ID (can be a reply or original tweet)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page (optional)

Note: Pagination size varies due to Twitter API limitations.
The number of tweets per page is not fixed.

Cost: 2.0 credits per request (variable tweets per page)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns the full thread context with pagination support.

Advanced search for tweets with powerful query syntax

Supports advanced Twitter search operators for precise tweet discovery.

Parameters:
- query: Search query with advanced operators (required)
  Examples:
  - "AI" OR "Twitter" from:elonmusk since:2021-12-31_23:59:59_UTC
  - "#bitcoin" min_faves:100
  - "python" filter:verified
  See: https://github.com/igorbrigadir/twitter-advanced-search

- query_type: "Latest" (chronological) or "Top" (most relevant)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page (optional)

Cost: 2.5 credits per request (up to 20 tweets)
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns search results with pagination support.

Search for Twitter users by keyword

Parameters:
- query: Search keyword (required)
- cursor: Pagination cursor for next page (optional)

Cost: 1.5 credits per request
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Returns matching users with pagination support.

Get tweet mentions by user screen name
Each page returns exactly 20 mentions
Order by mention time descending
Cost: 1.0 credit per request
Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds

Gross Profit Margin = Gross Profit / Revenue
Operating Margin = Operating Income / Revenue
Net Profit Margin = Net Income / Revenue
EBITDA Margin = EBITDA / Revenue
Return on Assets = Net Income / Total Assets
Return on Equity = Net Income / Total Equity
Return on Invested Capital = NOPAT / Invested Capital
Return on Capital Employed = EBIT / Capital Employed
Basic Earning Power = EBIT / Total Assets
Cash Return on Assets = Operating Cash Flow / Total Assets
Cash Return on Equity = Operating Cash Flow / Total Equity
Cash Return on Invested Capital
Current Ratio = Current Assets / Current Liabilities
Quick Ratio = (Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities
Cash Ratio = (Cash + Marketable Securities) / Current Liabilities
Absolute Liquidity Ratio = Cash / Current Liabilities
Defensive Interval = Quick Assets / Daily Operating Expenses
Working Capital = Current Assets - Current Liabilities
Working Capital Ratio
Asset Turnover = Revenue / Total Assets
Fixed Asset Turnover = Revenue / Fixed Assets
Inventory Turnover = COGS / Average Inventory
Receivables Turnover = Revenue / Average Receivables
Payables Turnover = COGS / Average Payables
Days Sales Outstanding = (Receivables / Revenue) * 365
Days Inventory Outstanding = (Inventory / COGS) * 365
Days Payables Outstanding = (Payables / COGS) * 365
Cash Conversion Cycle = DSO + DIO - DPO
Operating Cycle = DSO + DIO
Working Capital Turnover = Revenue / Working Capital
Equity Turnover = Revenue / Total Equity
Debt to Equity = Total Debt / Total Equity
Debt to Assets = Total Debt / Total Assets
Debt to Capital = Total Debt / (Total Debt + Total Equity)
Equity Ratio = Total Equity / Total Assets
Equity Multiplier = Total Assets / Total Equity
Interest Coverage = EBIT / Interest Expense
EBITDA Interest Coverage = EBITDA / Interest Expense
Cash Coverage = Operating Cash Flow / Interest Expense
Net Debt to EBITDA
Long-term Debt to Equity
Financial Leverage = Total Assets / Total Equity
Capitalization Ratio = Long-term Debt / (Long-term Debt + Equity)
Debt Service Coverage Ratio = OCF / (Debt + Interest)
Year-over-Year Growth
Compound Annual Growth Rate
Sustainable Growth Rate = Retention Ratio * ROE
Internal Growth Rate = (ROA * Retention) / (1 - ROA * Retention)
Operating Cash Flow to Debt
Operating Cash Flow Ratio = OCF / Current Liabilities
Operating Cash Flow Margin = OCF / Revenue
Free Cash Flow = Operating Cash Flow - CapEx
Free Cash Flow Margin
Cash Conversion Quality = OCF / Net Income
CapEx to Operating Cash Flow
CapEx to Depreciation (maintenance vs growth)
Cash Coverage of Dividends = OCF / Dividends
Reinvestment Rate = CapEx / OCF
Price to Earnings Ratio
Price to Book Ratio
Price to Sales Ratio
Price to Cash Flow Ratio
Price to Free Cash Flow Ratio
PEG Ratio = P/E / Earnings Growth Rate
EV/EBITDA Ratio
EV/Revenue Ratio
EV/FCF Ratio
Earnings Yield = EPS / Price (inverse of P/E)
FCF Yield
Dividend Yield
Tobin's Q = Market Value / Replacement Cost
Accruals Ratio = (Net Income - OCF) / Total Assets
Sloan Accruals Ratio = (Net Income - OCF) / Total Assets
Earnings Persistence (autocorrelation of earnings)
Earnings Variability (coefficient of variation)
Cash Earnings Ratio = OCF / Net Income
Quality of Earnings = OCF / Net Income

get.description:
Browse CEIC indicator catalog (metadata)

**Credits:** 3 credits per request

**Returns:** Indicator name, unit, category, frequency, date range per country

parameters.name: frequency

schema.description: Filter by frequency (e.g. quarterly, monthly, annual)
schema.title: Frequency

parameters.description: Filter by frequency (e.g. quarterly, monthly, annual)

get.description:
Get list of unique countries in CEIC indicator catalog

**Credits:** 2 credits per request

Get list of unique categories in CEIC indicator catalog

**Credits:** 2 credits per request

Check CEIC database connectivity

**Credits:** Free

Get economic calendar events with flexible filtering

**Credits:** 5 credits per request

**Data Source:** ForexFactory and other financial calendars
**Date Range:** 2007-01-01 to 2026-02-27
**Total Events:** 200,000+

**Filters:**
- currency: Filter by currency code (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, CAD, NZD, CNY, CHF, etc.)
- impact: Filter by impact level (Low, Medium, High, Holiday)
- event_name: Search in event names (case-insensitive partial match)
- start_date: Filter events from this date/time onwards
- end_date: Filter events up to this date/time
- source: Filter by data source (forexfactory, etc.)
- limit: Maximum records (1-1000, default 100)
- offset: Skip records for pagination

**Returns:**
- List of economic calendar events with full details
- Total count of matching records
- Applied filters summary

**Event Fields:**
- event_datetime: When the event occurs
- currency: Currency affected by the event
- impact: Impact level on the market
- event_name: Name of the economic indicator/event
- actual: Actual value released
- forecast: Forecasted value
- previous: Previous value
- detail_url: Link to more details

parameters.name: currency

schema.description: Filter by currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP)
schema.title: Currency

parameters.description: Filter by currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP)

parameters.name: impact

schema.description: Filter by impact level (Low, Medium, High, Holiday)
schema.title: Impact

parameters.description: Filter by impact level (Low, Medium, High, Holiday)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of records to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return

Query Trading Economics economic calendar events

**Date Range:** Current week and upcoming days

**Filters:**
- country: ISO country code (US, DE, JP, GB, FR, CA, AU, IN, etc.)
- category: Event category (partial match)
- event: Event name search (partial match)
- start_date / end_date: Date range (YYYY-MM-DD)
- importance: Importance level (integer)
- limit / offset: Pagination

**Returns:** Economic events with actual, forecast, previous values
